title: String Operations
overview: Strings are everywhere in programming. Python gives you an incredibly rich set of tools to analyze, transform, and manipulate text.

blocks.title: Strings are Sequences
blocks.body: A string is a sequence of characters. You can access individual characters, slice portions, and measure length — just like lists.
bullets: Indexing: text[0] gets the first character
Slicing: text[1:4] gets characters at index 1-3
len(text) returns the number of characters
Negative indexing: text[-1] gets the last character

blocks.title: String Indexing and Slicing
blocks.code:
text = "Python"
print(text[0])
print(text[-1])
print(text[1:4])
print(text[:3])
print(text[::2])
print(len(text))

blocks.title: Explore a String
blocks.instructions: Create a string with your name. Print first char, last char, length, and first 3 chars.
blocks.hint: Use index 0 for first, -1 for last, len() for length, [:3] for slice.
blocks.expectedOutput:
First: L
Last: a
Length: 9
First 3: Lak

blocks.title: Powerful String Methods
blocks.body: Python strings have built-in methods for changing case, removing whitespace, finding text, and more.
bullets: strip() — removes whitespace from both ends
find(substring) — returns index or -1 if not found

blocks.title: Case Methods and Cleanup
blocks.code:
text = "  Hello, World!  "
print(text.upper())
print(text.lower())
print(text.strip())
print(text.strip().replace("World", "Python"))

msg = "hello.txt"
print(msg.endswith(".txt"))
blocks.expectedOutput:
HELLO, WORLD!  
  hello, world!  
Hello, World!
Hello, Python!
True

blocks.title: Searching in Strings
blocks.code:
sentence = "The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog"
print(sentence.find("fox"))
print(sentence.find("cat"))
print(sentence.count("the"))
print(sentence.lower().count("the"))

blocks.title: Splitting and Joining
blocks.body: Strings can be split into lists and lists can be joined into strings.
bullets: split() — splits a string into a list (default: space)
" ".join(list) — joins list items into a string

blocks.title: Split and Join
blocks.code:
csv_data = "Alice,Bob,Charlie,Diana"
names = csv_data.split(",")
print(names)

sentence = "Python is awesome"
words = sentence.split()
print(words)

rejoined = " - ".join(words)
print(rejoined)

blocks.title: Palindrome Checker
blocks.instructions: Ask for a word. Check if it reads the same forward and backward (ignore case).
blocks.hint: Use [::-1] to reverse the string. Use lower() to ignore case.
blocks.expectedOutput:
Enter a word: Racecar
Yes, it's a palindrome!

keyTakeaways.text: Strings are sequences — index, slice, and measure with len()
upper(), lower(), strip() change and clean strings
find(), startswith(), endswith(), count() search strings
split() breaks strings into lists; join() combines them
String reversal with [::-1] is useful for palindrome checks

whatYouLearned: How to index and slice strings like lists
How to change case and clean strings with methods
How to split and join strings
How to build a palindrome checker
